golden_cases.notes: Guild-order list state before selecting or verifying one order.
Guild-order detail state where submit or refresh may be considered.
Supporting self-select guild-order detail state where the first-round bonus custom-order card is focused and the custom-order list button is visible.
Supporting self-select guild-order list state where visible custom-order candidates can be inspected and one option may be selected truthfully.
Supporting insufficient-material state used to verify skip or refresh decisions honestly.
Supporting already-completed state used to verify skip decisions honestly.
Supporting post-submit result state used to verify successful submission honestly.

steps.success_condition: Guild-order list is visible and stable enough for fixed-UI inspection.
steps.failure_condition: Guild-order list cannot be reached through deterministic navigation.
steps.notes: Round 9 keeps navigation bounded to the fixed guild-order UI only.

steps.success_condition: The current order slot, visible requirement or self-select order state, and visible available-material state can be classified honestly.
steps.failure_condition: The current standard requirement, custom-order state, or visible quantity state remains ambiguous.
steps.notes: No freeform OCR-only inference is allowed in this first cut.

steps.success_condition: When the current slot is a self-select order, the visible candidate options can be inspected honestly from the bounded custom-order list.
steps.failure_condition: The custom-order list, visible option requirements, or visible available-material state remains ambiguous.
steps.notes: This step applies only when the current guild-order slot is the self-select custom order.

steps.success_condition: The task chooses submit, skip, or refresh from machine-readable material policy and visible evidence only, including self-select order candidates when present.
steps.failure_condition: No truthful bounded decision can be made from visible state and configured policy.
steps.notes: This step must not introduce crafting, gathering, or pathing as hidden dependencies.

steps.success_condition: The selected bounded decision path is applied without expanding beyond submit, skip, or refresh, including bounded self-select option picking when required before submission.
steps.failure_condition: Submit or refresh affordances are missing when the bounded decision requires them.
steps.notes: Skip remains an explicit no-action path that still records the reason honestly.

steps.success_condition: The task verifies the chosen submit, skip, or refresh outcome with truthful post-decision evidence, including custom-order selection outcomes when that path is taken.
steps.failure_condition: The bounded decision outcome cannot be verified from stable guild-order UI evidence.
steps.notes: Round 9 requires honest verification instead of assuming the action succeeded.

guild_order_material_policy.notes: Guild-order submission now includes the bounded self-select order path. The self-select order is a first-round bonus card, not a second-round mode. The task may inspect visible custom-order candidates and choose one truthful option using the same material-policy rules, but it still must not hide crafting, gathering, or pathing as dependencies.

decision_rules.submit: Visible standard requirements are allowed and sufficiently stocked, or a visible self-select option is chosen truthfully under the same material policy.
decision_rules.skip: Visible order is already completed, self-select support is unavailable or disabled, refresh is disallowed, or no truthful bounded action remains after policy evaluation.
decision_rules.refresh: Visible order or self-select option set is unsuitable and one bounded refresh remains allowed by policy.

guild_order_decision_contract.notes: Unknown order state should fail honestly instead of inventing a fourth hidden action path. Self-select orders stay inside the same submit / skip / refresh decision envelope as a first-round bonus path, must record the chosen option explicitly, and should preserve truthful failure reasons when an operator-selected candidate index is missing, blocked, or insufficient.

guild_order_visibility_contract.notes: Guild-order submit remains blocked until visible quantities, self-select option surfaces, and post-decision result states are truthful and deterministic.

notes: Round-9 specification-only blueprint for deterministic guild-order submit / skip / refresh logic using visible material evidence only.

task_manifest.entry_condition: Guild panel and guild-order list are reachable through deterministic menu navigation and the account belongs to a guild.
task_manifest.failure_condition: The order state, visible material evidence, or bounded verification path cannot be classified honestly.
task_manifest.name: Guild Order Submit

stop_conditions.message: Guild-order submit fixture exceeded task-side timeout.
Operator stop should abort guild-order submit immediately.
Expected guild-order anchors did not match stable UI.

task_manifest.success_condition: The task reaches a truthful submit, skip, or refresh outcome for either a standard or self-select guild order and records why that outcome was chosen.
